name: Ribadavia - return via Arnoia river and Frieira Reservoir
name_original: Ribadavia – vuelta por río Arnoia y Embalse de Frieira
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ribadavia are primarily characterized by their challenging terrain, featuring significant elevation changes. The region is shaped by river valleys, such as the Arnoia and Avia rivers, and includes reservoirs like Frieira. These routes often traverse varied landscapes, offering a mix of natural and historical points of interest. Best touring cycling routes around Ribadavia The most popular touring cycling route is Ribadavia - return via Arnoia river and Frieira Reservoir, a 21.0 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route follows the Arnoia river and passes by the Frieira Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is A Caniza - return via Pozas Melon - Ribadavia and Cortegada, a difficult 29.1 miles (46.8 km) path. This route connects several towns and features varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Leiro – loop via Avia river, San Amaro and Monastery of San Clodio de Leiro, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail leading through the Avia river valley and past historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 0 minutes. Touring cycling around Ribadavia is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and historical landmarks. The network offers options primarily for experienced touring cyclists due to the prevalence of difficult routes. The routes in Ribadavia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 140 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ribadavia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ribadavia?
A: There are 19 touring cycling routes around Ribadavia listed on komoot. These routes primarily feature challenging terrain with significant elevation changes, making them suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Ribadavia?
A: The touring cycling routes around Ribadavia are predominantly challenging, with 17 out of 19 routes rated as difficult. There are no easy routes, and only 2 are rated as moderate. This region is generally better suited for experienced cyclists looking for a demanding ride rather than beginners or families with young children.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ribadavia?
A: Ribadavia's touring cycling routes are characterized by significant elevation changes and varied landscapes. You'll often find yourself cycling through river valleys, such as those of the Arnoia and Avia rivers, and past reservoirs like Frieira. The terrain offers a mix of natural beauty and historical points of interest, but be prepared for climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Ribadavia?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018462767/leiro-loop-via-avia-river-san-amaro-and-monastery-of-san-clodio-de-leiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiro – loop via Avia river, San Amaro and Monastery of San Clodio de Leiro&lt;/a&gt; is a 27.7 km circular trail that takes you through the Avia river valley and past historical sites. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lonia Park – Millennium Bridge loop from Barbantes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the cycling paths?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter stunning waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6067935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourón Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. Routes often follow river valleys and pass by reservoirs such as the Frieira Reservoir, offering scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrelo de Miño Nautical Park&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful spot.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. Many routes incorporate historical and cultural points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018462767/leiro-loop-via-avia-river-san-amaro-and-monastery-of-san-clodio-de-leiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiro loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Monastery of San Clodio de Leiro. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Esperela&lt;/a&gt;, a historic bridge, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ribadavia?
A: Given the region's challenging terrain and the prevalence of river valleys, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be a good option, especially for routes that pass by reservoirs or rivers, providing opportunities to cool down, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Ribadavia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ribadavia are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, the combination of natural and historical sights, and the challenging nature of the rides, which provide a rewarding experience for those seeking a workout.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for touring cycling routes in Ribadavia?
A: Routes in Ribadavia vary in length, with many being between 25 km and 60 km. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017450819/ribadavia-return-via-arnoia-river-and-frieira-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadavia - return via Arnoia river and Frieira Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is 33.7 km with over 550 meters of elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018453398/a-caniza-return-via-pozas-melon-ribadavia-and-cortegada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Caniza - return via Pozas Melon - Ribadavia and Cortegada&lt;/a&gt; is 46.8 km with nearly 950 meters of ascent. Expect significant climbs on most routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by thermal springs or hot springs?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31886827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from Barbantes&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, challenging route of 57.6 km that specifically includes the Outariz Hot Springs, offering a unique point of interest during your ride.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not detailed for every route, many routes connect towns and villages, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018453398/a-caniza-return-via-pozas-melon-ribadavia-and-cortegada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Caniza route&lt;/a&gt; which passes through Ribadavia and Cortegada. These settlements typically offer opportunities for refreshments and breaks. It's advisable to check your route in advance for specific amenities.
tips: Hot springs near the Mino river. Free public access to parking. You can choose between a paid spa or a wild swim in the hot springs near the river.

Ribadavia is located at a strategic point in the Miño Valley, a wine trade route and an old Roman road where the Avia and Arnoia rivers meet the Miño. In the middle of a landscape surrounded by vineyards, the castle of Ribadaiva is a vestige of a defensive fortress dating from the Middle Ages.
Between the cooling shade of the riverside trees, this is a nice and easy path along the Miño river between Ribadavia and the Arnoia river month.

The Miño river is tamed by two hydroelectric dams between Ribadavia and Cortogada creating the Filgueira dam where the river water slows down. Crossing the OU-801 by the new bridge you can see the old abandoned Filgueira Bridge.
